ety: inf. n. of قرّ 'to dwell, to abide'; 'to rest,' &c.
def: s.m. Dwelling, residence; fixing (one's) abode (in), settling; resting; fixedness, fixity; permanence; consistency; stability, firmness, constancy; tenacity (of purpose); — rest, repose, quietness, quiet, peace, tranquillity; quietude, patient waiting, patience; — settlement, determination, confirmation; conclusion; ratification; agreement, engagement; — reality, certainty, truth
more: n. m. قر to rest. See ستهرتائي and iqrār , 1, 3. 1. Quietude; patience. 2. Fixedness; permanence. 3. Consistency; tenacity of purpose. qarār ānā , v. n. To be at rest, or at ease. qarār pānā , v. n. To be settled ( ṭhairnā , 7, 8). qarār pāyā . 1. Arose; based. 2. Decided; determined. 3. Provided; ruled; established; prescribed; laid down. qarār-dād . 1. Establishment; settlement. 2. Engagement; agreement; contract. qarār-dād-bāham . A private bargain. qarārdād-i-jurm , The formal charge of a crime read out to a prisoner on his trial. qarār dīyā gayā . Laid down; determined. qarār denā , v. a. 1. To determine (the issues in a case) ( ṭhānnā , 2). 2. To adjudge ( tajvīz k . 1, 2). qarār k., qarār dād k. , v. a. See iqrār k . 4. 1. To take one's stand ( thānnā ). 2. To make terms; conclude (an agreement.) qarār-vāqaī , adv. 1. Definitively; positively. 2. Effectively; effectually. qarār o …

(from قرّ Heb. To rest, to cool) s. m. 1. Rest, residence, tranquillity, quietness. 2. Firmness, stability. 3. Ratification, agreement, engagement. 4. Waiting, patience. قرار داد ḳarār-dād, An engagement, contract, agreement. قراردينا ḳarār denā, To fix, to establish. قرارواقعي ḳarār-wāḳiʼīﻌ, adv. Effectively, effectually, efficaciously, truly.
subs: (-ko), 'Rest, &c. to come' (to), to get or find rest, &c., to be at rest, or ease, &c.; to be or become still or quiet
v.n. To be or become fixed or settled, or at rest; to be agreed upon; to be determined, or decided; to be established, to be laid down, or ruled, or provided; to obtain; to be based (on); to arise

(prob. contrac. fr. qarār-dāda, 'settled, established,' &c.), s.m. Settlement, establishment; confirmation; conclusion; ratification; agreement, engagement, contract; bargain; the specification, or formal charge (of a crime, &c.); — qarār-dād karnā, v.t. To settle, confirm, conclude, &c.
(-ko), To fix, establish, settle; to determine, decide; to lay down; to adjudge; to receive, admit (as correct, e.g. ṣaḥīḥ qarār denā)
v.t. To settle, establish, confirm, ratify, conclude (a bargain, treaty, &c.); — v.n. To establish oneself (on); to take (one's) stand (on); — to agree, promise ( = iqrār karnā)
s.m. A written engagement, &c. ( = iqrār-nāma, q.v.)
adv. Definitively; positively; — effectually, efficaciously; truly, accurately rightly, in a proper manner.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قَرَار (qarār), from Arabic قَرَار (qarār).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Hard, firm, rigid, stiff, tight; sturdy, strong, muscular; crusty; crisp; well-baked (bread, &c.); high-priced, dear, heavy (as price); of full weight, new (coin)
more: adj. S. कर्कर 1. Hard; stiff; rigid. 2. Muscular; well-set; sturdy ( takṛā ). Ab tak woh karārā banā huā hai . 3. Crisp; well-baked; short-crusted. 1. Karārī roṭī . 2. Karāre hole chāb! Street cry. 3. Reoṛī, paṭṭī gajak karārī! Street cry. 4. Arī taṅbolan! koī karārā sā chhānṭ le! Double entendre. Fair tobacconist, come! a crisp one (strong man) choose! 4. High-priced; dear. 5. New full-weight (coin). Karāre rupaë. karārā-pan , n. m. 1. Hardness. 2. Crispness. 3. Muscular strength. karāre dam , adv. 1. Unbending; unyielding. 2. Strong; firm. 3. Unflinching; resolute.
n. m. The perpendicular bank of a river; a precipice.
subs: adj. Strong, firm; unyielding; unflinching, resolute.

def: Rus. n. m. Crackling; rattle. 1. Karārī roṭī āchhī karaṛ karaṛ chabai saë revṛī ḍhāl . Rus. Crisp bread between the teeth as reoṛī sweetmeats goes 'crack crack.' 2. Sarar sarar woh karar karar kaṛī toṛī bakhtar kī, Kete sipāhan ke ghāo kare bhak bhak, kete sipāhin ne rāh li ghar kī! Sākhā . A ballad. Swords cut and slash amain, mail shirts are cleft in twain! Name of the place in عراق iﻌrāḳ, where Ḥusain son of Aﻌlī was killed and buried; hence, the word is sometimes applied in India to the shrines or burying places of the Muhammadans.

subs: The heart to bound or leap (with joy, &c.); to be overjoyed; the heart to palpitate
To lose heart, to be anxious or distracted
The heart to come to the mouth; to be exhausted with excessive vomiting
(-ke), To be wounded in the heart; to suffer a heavy shock or blow
To suffer jealousy or envy; to repine
(-kā, or merā, &c.), To be deeply wounded, to be cut to the quick
(kā), The heart to burst; to feel pity or compassion; to be disturbed with grief; to burst with envy or jealousy
(-kā), 'The heart to be fresh'; to be at ease, to be well off
'To press the heart and weep,' to weep bitterly
To suppress (one's) feelings; to repress grief
To satisfy the heart's longing, to obtain (one's) wish; to get ease
(-kā), 'The heart to burn,' to suffer sorrow, to be heart-sore, to mourn
(-ko), To keep next to the heart, to hold very dear, to love exceedingly, to cherish; to caress, embrace
The heart to tremble or palpitate; to feel afraid; to suffer cold
Liver-complaint; heart-disease: kalejā muṅh-ko ānā = kalejā ulaṭnā; — (also) to feel sick (at the sight of), to loathe, abhor
(-ko), To cherish in the heart, to love or esteem exceedingly
or kaleje-khāʼī, s.f. A witch who devours the livers of human beings.

n. m. S. कर्षण drawing; Pālī, kaḍḍhatī . 1. Drawings; advances. 2. A decoction of various medicinal roots and herbs, esp. given to a woman after miscarriage. Garabh-pāt meṅ kāṛhā dete haiṅ .
part. adj. (f. -ī), Drawn, drawn out, extracted, &c. (see kaṛhnā); fresh-drawn (milk, &c.); overboiled (milk)

(from قرح) s. m. A wound, a pimple, sore, ulcer. قرحه هونا ḳarḥa honā, To fester.

def: s.m. = kaṛāhā, q.v.

n. f. A dish consisting of the meal of pulse ( chanā or cicer arietinum ) with spices and sour milk. 1. Kaṛhī meṅ koëlā . Prov. In the dish a coal. (The coal is put in as a protection from the evil eye). 2. Bhāī! kaṛhī kā khaṭās aur guṛ kā miṭhās sar- nāṅo saë . Rus. My friend! the acid of kaṛhī and the sweetness of jaggery are notorious. 3. Patlī jahāṅ kisī ne dāl aur kaṛhī pakāī Makkhī ne vahīṅ bolī ā ūṅṭ kī bulāī! Nazīr. When dāl, pulse meal, and kaṛhī watery are and thing. The throat as camel's gurgles with a fly fallen in. 4. Terī kaṛhī karūṅ! A curse. May you be crunched (like a curry)! bāsī kaṛhī meṅ ubāl ānā , v. n. To boil with the rage of an old man.
s.m. (rustic) = karhānā, q.v. s.m. (f. ?), The loins, the waist; the buttocks.

ety: kāṛh˚ = Prk. कड्ढ(इ), fr. कड्ढ = S. कृष्ट, p.p.p. of rt. कृष्
def: v.t. To draw, drag, pull, &c.; to draw out, draw forth (a sword, &c.), draw off; to extract; to take out or off or forth; to pull out, to extricate; to strip off (the skin, &c.); to clear (land); to drive out or away; to displace; to draw, trace, form (letters, or lines, or figures); to form, fashion; to delineate; to paint; to work figures (on cloth, or in needlework), to embroider; — to invent, contrive, devise, project, strike out (a fashion, way, mode, &c.); to draw, derive, gather, obtain from; to gain by labour, to earn, make; to take up (money), to draw, to borrow; — to bring out, to introduce (as a subject of discourse); to bring out or make out (a meaning, &c.); to elicit; to deduce, to infer; to bring to light, to detect, discover, apprehend (faults, or excellences, or properties); to train, to break in (a beast); — to rake out or up, to scratch out or up; to explore, trace, search out; — to drag out or along, to bear or undergo (grievous days or circumstances, or pain, or affliction); — to wait upon, to serve, minister to (the sick, &c.)
more: v. a. 1. To draw forth (as, a sword), to draw, draw off, skin, take out. 2. To delineate, draw, paint, to work flowers on cloth.

ety: kaṛh˚ = Prk. कड्ढ(इ); fr. Prk. कड्ढ = S. कृष्ट, rt. कृष्
def: v.n. To be drawn, be drawn out, be pulled out, be taken out, be extracted; to be drawn, be delineated, be painted; to be figured, to be embroidered; — to come out, issue, rise, spring; to get out, escape, to slip forth or away.
more: v. n. To be drawn, pulled out or extracted, to be delineated, drawn or painted (as, a picture), to escape, rise, slip, issue, spring.

def: v. a. See bāhar k., ījād k . and اينچنا (1-3, 5, 7). 1. To draw out or forth. 2. To extract; express; take out. 3. ( Slang ) To bring forth; give birth to. 4. To borrow; contrive. 5. To earn; get. 6. To bring out; make out (the meaning). 7. To deduce; infer. 8. To take away; steal. Dabī āg mhārī kāḍh-ke le gaī, maryo khasam paṛausan kā! Kit sūṅ dūṅgī jib pīvegā hokā bābū chhoran kā? Wom. Rus. song. My neighbour, (may her husband die!) has stolen my ash-pent fire, Now how shall I prepare the pipe that soothes my children's sire? 9. To clear (land). 10. To detect; find out; discover. Bhūl kāṛhnā . 11. To project; delineate; draw; sketch; trace; copy ( eṅchnā ). 12. To break in (a horse for harness). 13. To displace; supersede. 14. To drive away; eject. 15. To do needle work; ply one's needle. Laṛkī kashidah kāṛhtī hai . kaṛ'han , Embroidery; tissue.
n. m. Thatching grass. (?).

ety: Prk. कड्ढओ (with w inserted); S. कृष्ट+कः
def: s.m. A loan, debt; a premium on a loan, a deduction from the sum lent.
more: (from کڙهنا) s. m. A loan, a debt: a premium on a loan: a deduction from the sum lent.

def: n. 1. A loan. 2. A deduction from the sum lent.
v. a. Caus. of کارَهنا q. v.
v.t. To cause to be drawn out or off; to cause to be extracted; to have or get drawn, or delineated, or embroidered, &c.
s.m. A reader (esp. of the Qorʼān).
more: Hin. n. m. opp. of A. sāme . A reader, esp. of the Qurān.
(part. act. of قرأ Heb. To read) s. m. A reader, especially of the ḳurʼān. (plur. قاريين ḳāriyīn ).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قاری (qārī), from Arabic قَارِئ (qāriʔ).

def: adj. Operating, effectual, penetrating, deep, mortal (as a wound).
more: adj. Mortal ( qātil ). Birah kā tīr kārī jiske lāge, Na jīve nā mare, sove na jāge . Qutbī Shāh. Whom love's too lethal dart o'ertakes Nor lives nor dies, nor sleeps nor wakes. kārī-zakhm, zakhm-i-kārī . A mortal wound.
(Europeans), n. f. (Cor. of H. kaṛhī or tarkārī ) Curry. kārī bhāt (Europeans), n. m. Curry and rice.
(from کار) adj. Effectual, operating, penetrating. کاري زخم kār-ī zaḵẖm (or zaḵẖm-i-kārī ) A deep wound, a mortal wound.
used in compos. Making, maker, doer.

def: adj. & s.m. (used as last member of compounds), Making, doing, performing, causing; — maker, doer, performer, agent, cause, &c.
s.f. (dialec.) A blade (of grass, or corn, &c.); a bit of straw or stick, a fescue; a chip
more: s. (dakh.) Blade (of corn or grass) fescue ( Harris ).

ety: dim. of kaṛā, and = S. कट+इका
def: s.f. A ring or circle (of metal), link (of a chain); a window-hook, door-hook (which closes by entering into a staple); a staple; a manacle, handcuff, fetter; a verse, or the chorus (of a song); — a small beam, a rafter.
more: s. f. 1. A rafter, a beam. 2. A ring used to manacle or to fetter with.
s. (dakh. see کڙا kaṛā ) A link (of a chain: Harris ).

def: s. (dakh.) See کچرا kachrā.
(in poetry, and old H.) = kar, q.v. s.m. Bottom, or depth (of a well, &c.); a hollow, cavity, low ground; a gulf, an abyss
subs: s.m. Depth of a river; trough of the sea; the sea
wety: Borrowed from the nominative singular of Sanskrit करिन् (karin), from कर (kara) + -इन् (-in). कर (kara) is from the root कृ (kṛ), while the suffix -इन् (-in) is from Proto-Indo-European *-iHnos. Related to کَر (kar, “hand, elephant's trunk”).

ety: corr. of English 'curry,' which is prob. corr. fr. kaṛhī, or tarkārī

s.m. An excellent elephant; the beautiful or chief elephant, an epithet of Gaṇeśa (in allusion to his face).
more: n. f. S. कठ to cover. 1. Trial; hardship; suffering. 2. A rafter; beam. 3. A link (of a chain). 4. A manacle; handcuff. 5. A verse, or the chorus of a song.
wety: Borrowed from the nominative singular of Sanskrit करिन् (karin), from कर (kara) + -इन् (-in). कर (kara) is from the root कृ (kṛ), while the suffix -इन् (-in) is from Proto-Indo-European *-iHnos. Related to کَر (kar, “hand, elephant's trunk”).

def: adj. Near (in point of time, place, or connexion), nigh, neighbouring, adjacent; akin, relative; — adv. Nearly, almost, about
more: adv. قرب drawing near. Adjacent; neighbouring ( pās 1). qarīb-ul-ikhtitām . Near the end or close. qarīb-ul-fahm, qarīb-ul-qayās, qarīn-i-aql . adj. 1. Conceivable; intelligible; comprehensible. 2. Reasonable; presumable; colorable. qarīb-i-marg . On the point of death; dying. qarīb, yā qarīn-ul-vuqu . Imminent; impending. qarīb pahuṅchnā , v. n. 1. See pās ānā . 2. To draw near; gain ground. qarīb-tar . Nearer; immediately; next to. qarīb-qarīb, anqarīb ; H. pās pās , adv. Very near. qarīb-kar , A. H. adv. Approximately ( pās pās ). qarīb kī rishte-dārī , n. f. Near relationship.
(from قرب) adv. Near, neighbouring, nigh, almost, about, akin, relative.
subs: On the point of completion; near the end or close
or qarībuʼl-qiyās, adj. Within the reach of the understanding or conception; conceivable, intelligible; comprehensible; reasonable; presumable
or qarīb-ě-marg, On the point of death, dying
adj. On the point of happening; impending, imminent

more: n. m. S. करीर Pālī kari'ro . A thorny shrub, growing in deserts and fed upon by camels; the caper bush; Capparis aphylla , Wat. Kābul meṅ mevā bhaī, Brij meṅ bhaī karīl . Prov. Kābul for fruit, and Brij for Karīl!
n. m. See ṭhīkrā , 5. kaṛyal javān , A bony person.

def: s.m. = karīr, q.v. s.m. Shoot of a bamboo; — a thorny leafless shrub, the caper bush, Capparis aphylla (which grows in deserts, and is eaten by camels); the fruit of this plant.
adj. Noble; magnificent, grand; generous, munificent, liberal, bountiful; benign, benevolent, beneficent, kind, courteous, gracious, merciful, forgiving; — an epithet of God, the Merciful Being.
more: adj. 1. Bountiful, gracious (God). 2. Liberal; generous. Sakhī karīm paṛe eṛiyāṅ ragaṛte haiṅ, Bakhīl mūsloṅ se motiyoṅ ko chhaṛte haiṅ . The times out of joint. Nazīr. The generous-hearted rub their heels and prostrate lie, While misers sifting pearls as corn threshed out you see.
(fem. کريمه karīma, from کرم) adj. Bountiful, magnificent, gracious, liberal; an epithet of God. (Pers. plur. کريمان).
wety: Borrowed from Arabic كَرِيم (karīm).

def: (for A. کريمة karīmat, fem. of karīm), adj. f. = karīm.

ety: karīm, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Liberality, bounty, beneficence, grace, &c. (see karīm).
s.m. (f. -ṇī), lit. 'Having a hand or trunk'; an elephant ( = karī).
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit किरण (kiraṇa). First attested in c. 1518 as Middle Hindi کرن (krn /⁠kiran⁠/).

ety: v.n. fr. قرن 'to join,' &c.; see qarn
def: adj. & s.m. Joined, conjoined, connected, next, contiguous, adjoining; adhering; akin, related, allied; — a friend, companion, associate; an equal; a contemporary; one of the same age
more: adj. See qarīb . qarīn-i-insāf . Just; equitable. qarīn-i-aql thā, yā qarīn-i-qayās thā . It is expected. qarīn-i-maslahat, yā salāh . Advisable; expedient.
adj. Conjoined, contiguous, connected; equal, associate, friend. گردون قرين gardūn-ḳarīn, adj. Joined or reaching to the skies. نصرقرين nuṣrat-ḳarīn, adj. Victorious.
subs: or qarīn-ě-ʻaql, adj. = qarībuʼl-fahm, q.v.s.v. qarīb
adj. Joined or reaching to the skies

ety: for A. قرينة qarīnat, fem. fr. qarīn, q.v.
def: s.m. Context; cause; conjecture, presumption; general tenour; symmetry; similarity, analogy, correspondence; connexion, arrangement, order; method of procedure, way, mode, manner
subs: adv. From the context; from the general tenour; by analogy; in order.

def: n. m. 1. Way; mode ( ḍhang , 1). 1. Is tere qarīne ko āg lage! Mah. Wom. 3. Kisī tarah se na thī dil meṅ rāh kīne ko, Na jānte the qarīne, na be-qarīne ko, Gale lipaṭte to kyā kyā ragaṛte sīne ko, Masaktī cholī ko logoṅ se chhup-ke sīne ko, Woh tāge baṭtā thā, aur maiṅ suī pirotā thā . Primeval innocence. Nazīr. No malice in the heart whate'er had sway then, From seemly and unseemly nought discerned then, Arms twined round necks, and breast 'against breast close rubbed then! In breast encounters secret jackets torn then. While she would thread it I the needle plied then. 2. Arrangement; system; order. 3. Context; correspondence; connexion; analogy. qarīne se , adv. 1. In order (tartīb se) . 2. From the context; from the general tenor. PHR. Putting this and that together. 3. By analogy. qarīne se lagānā, qarīnah ba qarīnah rakhnā , v. a. To arrange; put in order. kisī qarīne se yaqīn h. , v. n. To have reason to believe; to know or conclude from certain circumstances.
more: (from قرن) s. m. 1. Context, conjecture, similarity, analogy, symmetry, correspondence, connexion, regularity, order. 2. Cause.

def: s.f. A female elephant (see karin, karī).
s.m. Shoot of a bamboo; — a thorny leafless shrub, the caper bush, Capparis aphylla (which grows in deserts, and is eaten by camels); the fruit of this plant.
more: Name of a shrub. See کريل.

s.f. = P کارستان kār-istān, s.m. See s.v. kār.

Public or Government service; official functions. ba-kār-i-sarkār yā malikah muazzamah . On Her Majesty's Service. kār-shinās , adj. Experienced. kār-i-zurūrī , n. f. Urgent business or affairs. kār-i-savāb , An act of piety which will take one to Heaven. kār-i-farz , n. m. Imperative or bounden duty. kār-farmāī , n. f. Execution; administration. kār-kardah , adj. Experienced; trained.

To build a fort; to fortify (a place)

To take refuge in a fort; to keep in a fortified place (an army)

s.f. A siege-gun; siege-battery; a very large cannon
s.f. The garrison of a fort.

ety: fr. kaṛā, 'hard,' &c.; ˚al = Prk. अडो = S. लः

s.f. The plant Creyat, Justicia paniculata (used in medicine, &c.; it forms the basis of the well-known tincture called the Drogue amere).
more: n. S. किराततिक्त The Creyat or justicia paniculala used in medicine, etc. forming the basis of the well-known tineture called the Drogue amere .

def: s.m. Work, action, business, affair; profession (see kāj and kārya); aim, end, object, motive; cause, origin; — marriage, nuptials
more: n. m. S. कार्य्य 1. See kāj , (2, 3). 2. See arth 1, & irādah 1, 2, 4. 1. Sāīṅ! apne chitt kī bhūl na kahiye koë, Tab lag man meṅ rākhiye jab lag kāraj hoë . Girdhar. Dear Sir! your slips in your own mind fast locked conceal! Until your end is gained your blunders ne'er reveal! 2. Kāraj sidh hoṅ! Benediction. May your affairs prosper! 3. Marriage; nuptials. Unkī laṛkī kā kāraj hai. kāraj rachnā , v. a. To celebrate a marriage.
s. m. Business, affair, work, action, profession.
subs: s.f. Attainment of an object, accomplishment, success

def: s.m. A crab; the sign Cancer; — a white horse
more: n. m. 1. A crab. 2. The sign Cancer.
s. m. 1. A crab. 2. The sign Cancer.

s.m. Hail ( = karak); stone.
more: n. m. 1. A war song; martial music. 2. A ballad; epic; lay; pastoral. Kaṛkā sohe pālī ne, bārā sohe mālī ne . Rus. The epic suits the shepherd's song, for water-drawers the refrain long.

def: s.m. = kaṛkā, q.v. s.m. Hail ( = karak); stone.

def: adv. With a cracking sound, crack-crack; crunch-crunch, &c.
subs: To chew with a cracking sound, &c.

ety: v.n. fr. قرقر 'to coo; to laugh,' &c.
def: s.m. The demoiselle crane, Ardea virgo (syn. kaṛākul).
more: s. m. The name of a bird. (Ardea virgo).

def: s.m. The Numidian crane, Lanius excubitor; the Demoiselle crane, or small coolen.
s.m. Burst of laughter; immoderate laughter ( = qahqaha).
more: (corrupt. of قہقہه) s. m. Laughing immoderately.

def: adj. = karkarā, q.v. s.m. The demoiselle crane, Ardea virgo (syn. kaṛākul).
adj. & s.m. Hard, harsh, unkind, cruel; hard, stiff; crisp; dry, dried up; unsound, false, counterfeit, base (coin); — a bad coin.
more: n. m. S. कर्कर 1. A bad coin. 2. A bird; the Demoiselle crane or small Coolen of Europeans.
s. m. 1. Bad coin. 2. Name of a bird. (Numidian crane).

def: s.f. Cracking, snapping; creaking; grating.

ety: kaṛkaṛ˚, prob. by redupl. of S. कर्द; cf. kaṛaknā
def: v.n. To give forth a crackling sound, to crackle (as oil or butter when boiling, &c.); to chatter (as the teeth); to cackle, to cluck (as a laying hen); — v.t. To cause to crackle; to boil (oil or ghī); to melt down (fat); to grind or gnash (the teeth)
subs: v.t. To boil (oil, &c.); to melt down (fat).

ety: fr. S. कड redupl.; or fr. S. कटकटा
def: v.n. To cackle (as a hen); to crackle; to make a noise as a rat when burrowing under ground.
more: Sant. n. m. To cackle (a hen).
v. n. To make a noise, as a rat when burrowing under ground.
v. n. To give a crackling sound; as oil or butter when boiling. Ghī kaṛkaṛā lo!
v. n. S. कुर 1. To cluck; cackle (a laying hen). 2. To rack; tear. 3. To murmur; grumble. 4. To grudge; stint.

def: s. (dakh.) A clap (of thunder).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā) = karkaś, q.v.
more: adj. Harsh, violent, sharp, obdurate, contumelious.

def: adj. (f. -ā) & s.m. Hard, harsh, rough; intangible; violent, passionate; quarrelsome; contumelious; obdurate, unkind, unfeeling, pitiless, cruel; miserly; sharp, piercing; — a harsh man, a violent person, &c.
s.f. = karkaśā, q.v.
more: n. f. S. कर्कशा A termagant; scold; a quarrelsome woman. karka'sā , adj. Quarrelsome; litigious. 1. Jis ghar hoë karkasā nārī, us ghar hoë din din khuārī . Rus. Where termagant has sway the house decays each day. 2. Dhan dhan, purakh, tore bhāg! karaksā nār mili, Sove saverī, uṭhe averī, dho-pahar dāl rāṅdhe, Chhorā chhorī roṭī māṅgeṅ, rāṛ khasam se māṅḍe . Rus. Wom. song. All hail! man! blessed fate! linked to a scolding wife, Who early sleeps, late wakes. with midday's breakfast strife, The children cry for bread, her husband rows the wife. 3. Tere se maiṅ akelā bhalā, mat miliyo karaksā nār! Kachchī, pakkī roṭī pove, khaṛā ṭapaktā sāg, Ghar-vālā rūkā detā 'roṭī poī ki nāṅhīṅ ? Better apart than live with you, no termagant I'll wed! Her bread unbaked, her potherbs hard and watery 'fore you spread, The housefolk hungry crying keep 'prepared yet is our bread?'

def: s.f. A violent or passionate woman, a termagant, a scold, a shrew
s.m. Roughness, harshness; sternness; hardness; firmness, solidity.
s.m. A crab; the sign Cancer; — the Numidian crane, Lanius excubitor.
more: s. m. 1. A crab. 2. The sign Cancer.
wety: Borrowed from English cricket.

def: conj. part. &c. See s.v. kar. s.m. Bottom, or depth (of a well, &c.); a hollow, cavity, low ground; a gulf, an abyss
subs: s.m. Depth of a river; trough of the sea; the sea

def: s.m. A kind of gem or precious stone.
s.m. = kaṛkhā, q.v.

s.m. A loud shout; a song of triumph, a pæan; a war-song (to encourage combatants); the encouraging soldiers at the time of battle by pointing out the good effects of steadiness and valour, and by extolling the deeds of former heroes; commemoration.
more: s. m. Encouraging the soldiers in time of battle, by pointing out the good effects of steadiness and valour, extolling the actions of former heroes, &c.: war-song to encourage combatants.

ety: kaṛkhā, or kaṛkā + ait = Prk. आइंतो = आवेंतो = S. आपयन् (the termination of the pres. part., with the caus. augment āpi)
def: s.m. An officer in Indian armies whose business it is to encourage the soldiers by the exhortations called kaṛkhā, a kind of Indian Tyrtaeus; a bard (bhāṭ).
more: s. m. A kind of bard, an officer in Indian armies whose business it is to encourage the soldiers by the exhortations called کڙکها kaṛkhā.

def: s.m. The bael-tree, Ӕgle marmelos, and its fruit; the plant Momordica mixta; the sugar-cane; name of a Nāga or serpent.
n. m. A director; manager; assignee; a manager of a minor's estate; a representative; an attorney.
adj. & s.m. Uxorious; — an uxorious husband.
(For this and other comp. verbs of which the first member is the rt. or conj. part. kar, see s.v. karnā.
more: 1. v. a. To effect, to settle. 2. H. (corrupt. of چلانا chillānā ) v. n. To shriek.

def: s.m. Action act. deed, proceeding, performance, work, business, occupation, office; duty, obligation; moral conduct; religious observance, any religious action or rite (as sacrifice, ablution, funeral obsequies, &c.); moral duty or obligation (imposed by peculiarities of tribe or caste, occupation, &c.); (in Gram.) the accusative case; — fate (the certain consequence of previous acts), destiny, fortune, lot, portion; — product, result, effect
subs: (karmā = karma), adj. & s.m. Devout, virtuous; fortunate; fortuitous, accidental; — a devout person, &c.
(˚ma+adh˚), adj. (f. -ā), Subject to destiny, fated, destined
(˚ma+āg˚), part. adj. Descended, or inherited, in regular succession; handed down, traditional
s.f. 'The land or region of religious actions,' an epithet of Bharata, Airāvata, and Videha; — karam-bhog, s.m. The fulfilling of destiny; experiencing the consequences of actions
(-kā), Fate to prove adverse, to be unfortunate, have bad luck
(-kā), (One's) fortune or luck to take a favourable turn, to be in luck
s.m. An inferior officer to collect the revenue from a certain division of a village, a village accountant
adj. & s.m. Dismissed, or a person dismissed, from office
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Corrupt in action, wicked in practice, immoral, disreputable; — a low, immoral person
vulg. karam-rekhā, or karam-rekh, s.f. The mark or line of fate, any mark on the body by which one's fortune is ascertainable; destiny, fate, fortune
s.f. Accomplishment of an act, success; efficiency
adj. & s.m. Assiduous, laborious, painstaking; — a skilful or clever workman
vulg. karam-sīl, adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Assiduous, laborious, industrious, persevering; — an industrious person, &c.
s.f. or karm-śīlatva, s.m. Assiduity, industry, perseverance; — karm-kār, s.m. Worker, workman, artisan, mechanic; a blacksmith; a bull
(f. -ikā), and karm-kārī (f. -iṇī), adj. & s.m. Doing any act or work, working, carrying on a business; operative, effectual; — a worker, workman, agent, operative
s.m. 'What is marked or written by fate,' destiny, &c. ( = karm-rekhā)
s.m. The body of religious ceremonies commanded by Hindū law or established by custom, that department of the Veda which relates to ceremonial acts and sacrificial rites
adj. & s.m. Rendered low or vile by fate, unfortunate, miserable, wretched; — an unlucky wight, &c.
s.m. (f. -trī), s.m. An agent, attorney, ambassador; a locum tenens; a Hindū priest
s.m. Any property resulting from human acts

s.f. The prior or practical division of the Mīmāṅsā philosophy (so called because it relates to works, or religious observances undertaken for specific works; it may be described as a critical commentary on the first or ritual portion of the Veda)
vulg. karam-nāsā, s.f. Name of a river between Benares and Bihār (so. called because contact with its water is supposed to destroy the merit of works)
s.f. A verbal noun indicating the object; — the past passive participle
s.m. lit. 'The ripening of actions'; the good or evil consequences in this life of human acts performed in previous births; the consequences of actions
adj. & s.m. Luckless, unfortunate, wretched; — a luckless wight, &c.
s.m. The fourth and present age of the world, the iron age (syn. kali-yug)

(˚ma+in˚), s.m. An organ of action (opp. to buddhīndriya or 'perceptive organs of sense'; five are reckoned, viz. the hand, the foot, the larynx or organ of the voice, the organ of generation, and the anus).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کَرَم (karam), from Arabic كَرَم.

def: adj. कर्म and धर्म 1. Devout; virtuous. 2. Accidental; fortuitous. Karmā-dharmī saudā hai .
adj. 1. Devout, virtuous. 2. Fortunate. 3. Accidental.
s.f. An act, deed (see karma).
s.m. A grating or cracking sound (as of a dog gnawing a bone); crunching; murmuring, grumbling

v. n. 1. To gnaw (as a dog gnaws a bone). 2. Rus . To crackle; crunch. Sāṛā bārā ṭīkaṛ kaṛmaṛā gayā, to bhī nāhīṅ dhāpā . Rus. Twelve thick cakes this whoreson crackling ate, and his stomach's not yet filled. 3. Rus. To gnash one's teeth. Are! tauṅh kyoṅ karmaṛāyā binā-hak ? Rus. What are you murmuring there for nothing at all?
v.n. To crackle; to murmur, grumble, &c. ( = kaṛkaṛānā, and kuṛkuṛānā, qq.v.); — v.t. To gnaw; to crunch.
s.m. A name of the letter ओ o, or of its symbol.
s.m. (f. -tā), State of action; objectivity, the accusative state of any word.

def: s.m. A kind of vegetable.
more: s. m. Name of a vegetable.

r: karm-hīn, karam kā heṭā, yā hīnā
def: adj. Unfortunate; unlucky; wretched. Karam-hīn khetī kare, bail mare yā sūkhā pare . Prov. When luckless men their fields do sow or drought prevails or cattle go.

adj. & s.m. (f. -iṇī), Acting, active, busy, engaged in any work or business, occupied, laborious, diligent; performing a religious action; relating to any work or act; — favoured of fortune, fortunate; — a worker (esp. in the religious sense); an active or busy man, &c.
more: E. n. m. A caste of agriculturists in Eastern and Central Hindustān , being the same essentially as the Kunbīs of the West and South.
adj. Relating to any work, doing works, fortunate. ¶ (dakh.) s. f. Cause.

def: adj. (f. -ā) & s.m. Manufactured, made; worked; embroidered; intermixed with coloured thread (as cloth, &c.); — any variegated texture; a figured web.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Very active, assiduous, diligent; — a diligent person, &c.
n. m. The objective or accusative case.
s.m. A bow; a bow-shaped instrument; — bamboo.
s.m. Conjunction (of planets); — a space of ten years, or any multiple thereof up to one hundred and twenty years; a decade; a generation; an age; many years.
more: n. m. قرن joined. A period of 10 to 120 years, but generally a period of 12 years. Hameṅ to yahāṅ rahte qaran guzar gaë .
(from قرن ḳarana, To join) s. m. 1. Conjunction of the planets. 2. A period of (40, 10, 20, 30, 50, 60, 70, 80, 100, or 120) years. ( Ḳām. ).

def: v.t. = kāṛhnā, q.v. v.t. To draw, drag, pull, &c.; to draw out, draw forth (a sword, &c.), draw off; to extract; to take out or off or forth; to pull out, to extricate; to strip off (the skin, &c.); to clear (land); to drive out or away; to displace; to draw, trace, form (letters, or lines, or figures); to form, fashion; to delineate; to paint; to work figures (on cloth, or in needlework), to embroider; — to invent, contrive, devise, project, strike out (a fashion, way, mode, &c.); to draw, derive, gather, obtain from; to gain by labour, to earn, make; to take up (money), to draw, to borrow; — to bring out, to introduce (as a subject of discourse); to bring out or make out (a meaning, &c.); to elicit; to deduce, to infer; to bring to light, to detect, discover, apprehend (faults, or excellences, or properties); to train, to break in (a beast); — to rake out or up, to scratch out or up; to explore, trace, search out; — to drag out or along, to bear or undergo (grievous days or circumstances, or pain, or affliction); — to wait upon, to serve, minister to (the sick, &c.)
more: v. a. (dakh.) To draw or take out: see کاڙهنا kāṛhnā and نکالنا nikālnā.

def: s.m. A species of citron (called karan-phal in and about Kamāʼūn).
more: v. a. S. करण from कृ to do, Pr. kāūnā having made, kāūṅ, kiraī , it is done. 1. To do; act; work; make; perform; execute. 1. Bhail biyāh, mor karbe kā ? Bhoj. Prov. The knot is tied, what can you do? 2. Akelo pūt kamāī kare, ghar kā kare yā kachahrī kare ? Prov. One only son for breadwinner, the house or office work is he to do? 3. Kīyā aur kar na jānā, maiṅ hotī to kar dikhātī . Wom. Prov. To do and not know how! Had I been there I would have shown you how. 4. Apne kīye ko bhugto! Prov. Saying. As you do you must rue. 5. Kahnā āsān, karnā mushkil . Prov. Easy to say, hard to do. 2. To ply; use; wield. Sipāhī ne khūb talvār kī . 3. To commit; perpetrate. 4. To conduct; carry on (a business or trade); transact; discharge or fulfil (a duty). Ab to koṭhī kā kām ye karte haiṅ . 5. To hold a sitting or court; to hear a case. Do ghanṭe roz kachehrī karte haiṅ . 6. To contrive; manage. Aisā kar…
v. a. To do, make, form, perform, execute, effect, act, administer, avail, set, thrust, use, coire. s. m. Making, doing, &c. کرني والا karne-wālā, Maker, doer.
s. m. Name of a kind of citron. (Citrus).
s. m. indecl. A kind of trumpet, clarion, or bugle. See A. قرناي.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑀭𑁂𑀇 (karei), from Ashokan Prakrit 𑀓𑀭𑁄𑀢𑀺 (karoti), from Sanskrit क॒रोति॑ (karóti), from कृ॒णोति॑ (kṛṇóti), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *kr̥náwti, from Proto-Indo-European *kʷer- + *-néwti. Cognate with Punjabi ਕਰਨਾ (karnā), Nepali गर्नु (garnu), Persian کردن (kardan).

ety: kar˚ = Prk. कर(इ) = S. करो(ति), Vedic also कर(ति), rt. कृ
def: v.t. To do, act, perform, practise, execute, transact (business), conduct; to hold (a court, e.g. kaćahrī karnā); to keep, to open (a shop); to set; to set up; to achieve, effect, accomplish; to commit (a crime); to administer; to engage in; to work (a sum, &c.); to use, ply, wield (a sword, &c.); to make, form, frame; to appoint; to devise, contrive; to apply; to exert; to avail; to impart, confer, bestow (e.g. 'izzat karnā); to render, to cause to be or become, to turn or change (into); to prepare, do, dress (the hair, or food, &c.); to kill (an animal, for use, e.g. bakrī kī hai), to effect, work upon, influence, bring under (one's) influence or subjection (in this sense kar-denā is more common); to put (in, -meṅ), to insert; to thrust, thrust in, pierce; to put on, wear (e.g. dhotī karnā); to take (in marriage); to cohabit with, coire; to cry or call out, raise a cry of: (karnā, annexed to participles, adjectives, and substantives, forms what the grammarians call Nominal verbs, e.g. dūshit karnā, 'to make disgraced,' 'to vitiate'; ćaurā karnā, 'to make wide,' 'to widen'; ḵẖiyāl karnā, 'to think, fancy,' &c.; — karnā, following a verb. n. or gerund in the form of an uninflected perfect part., forms a Frequentative or Continuative compound, e.g. likhā karnā, 'to write frequently,' 'to continue or keep on writing,' 'to be in the habit of writing'; — the conj. part., as well as the root, kar, prefixed to certain verbs, forms intensives, or completives, &c., examples of which will be found in what follows) — kar-ānā, v.n. To return from doing, &c. (see kar-lānā)
subs: (-ko), To have done with, to rest or cease from; to do effectually or thoroughly; to do deliberately, or composedly, or unconcernedly
(-ko), 'To finish and depart,' to have done with
v.t. To show, exhibit; to make, to realize

v.t. (intens. of karnā), To do (to, -par); to render, to cause to be or become; to cause to be given or sent (with, — e.g. paćās toṛe uske-sāth kar-diye); to put (into), to transfer (from; — e.g. ghaṛe-kā pānī kalse-meṅ kar-de)
v.t. 'To do and see,' to try, test, put to the proof
(-ko), 'To do and leave,' to do (emphatically); to have done with
v.t. (intens. of karnā), To do, effect, accomplish; to form, contract; to take (into keeping, or in marriage); to procure, acquire, gain; to learn
or karan-hār, or karan-hārā, s.m. Doer, maker, originator, agent
or karne-jog, adj. Fit or proper to be done, fit, suitable; to be done, practicable.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑀭𑁂𑀇 (karei), from Ashokan Prakrit 𑀓𑀭𑁄𑀢𑀺 (karoti), from Sanskrit क॒रोति॑ (karóti), from कृ॒णोति॑ (kṛṇóti), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *kr̥náwti, from Proto-Indo-European *kʷer- + *-néwti. Cognate with Punjabi ਕਰਨਾ (karnā), Nepali गर्नु (garnu), Persian کردن (kardan).

ety: kaṛ˚, prob. S. काडय(ति), rt. कड्
def: v.t. To pierce, to perforate.
s.m. A trumpet, the large brass trumpet which sounds the bass.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑀭𑁂𑀇 (karei), from Ashokan Prakrit 𑀓𑀭𑁄𑀢𑀺 (karoti), from Sanskrit क॒रोति॑ (karóti), from कृ॒णोति॑ (kṛṇóti), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *kr̥náwti, from Proto-Indo-European *kʷer- + *-néwti. Cognate with Punjabi ਕਰਨਾ (karnā), Nepali गर्नु (garnu), Persian کردن (kardan).

def: s.m. The ear; handle (of a vessel); helm, rudder (of a ship or boat); — (in Pros.) a spondee; (in Geom.) hypothenuse (of a triangle); diagonal (of a square, or parallelogram, &c.); a chord (syn. watar); a secant; — lace cut into small pieces and put on a cap
subs: (˚ṇa+āś˚), adj. Following, or about, the diagonal (of a square, &c.)
s.m. Inflammation of the outer ear
s.f. The lobe of the ear; the outer and curving edge of the ear; a garland, or a string of jewels, pendant from the ear as an ornament
s.m. A disease of the ear, suppression of its excretion or wax, which is supposed to have dissolved and passed by the nose and mouth
s.m. A sort of fish, Ophiocephalus kurrawey; — (local) a citron ( = karnā)
vulg. karan-phūl, s.m. An ornament for the ear consisting of a hollow cone of gold to the base of which pearls are attached, and which is fixed as a pendant to the ear-ring
s.f. lit. 'The ear leech'; the Julus ( = karṇ-kīṭī, q.v.)
s.m. Helmsman, steersman; boatman; pilot; supercargo
s.m. Running of the ear; discharge of ichorous matter from the ear
or karṇ-kīṭā, s.f. 'Ear-worm,' the Julus; a small centipede
s.f. The lobe of the ear ( = karṇ-pālī)
s.m. A sort of sheat-fish, Silurus unitus
s.f. An epithet of Devī or Durgā in one of her forms or incarnations
s.m. The root of the ear; — a swelling near the ear, parotis
vulg. karan-bedhan, s.m. Piercing the ears
s.f. An instrument for piercing the ear.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit किरण (kiraṇa). First attested in c. 1518 as Middle Hindi کرن (krn /⁠kiran⁠/).

ety: A. qarn, 'a horn'+P. nāy or nāʼe, q.v.
def: s.f. A musical horn; the large brass trumpet which sounds the bass; a trumpet, bugle, clarion, &c.
more: n. m. Dim. qarnāī , n. f. A trumpet; horn; French horn.
s. (dakh.) A straight or curved musical instrument, a kind of French horn, made of brass, twelve feet long ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

ety: A. qarn, 'a horn'+P. nāy or nāʼe, q.v.
def: s.f. A musical horn; the large brass trumpet which sounds the bass; a trumpet, bugle, clarion, &c.

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کارنامه (kārnāma). By surface analysis, کار (kār, “action, deed”) + نامہ (nāma, “history”)

def: s.m. The country called the Carnatic (the name was anciently applied to the central districts of the peninsula, including Mysore); — karnāṭak, s.m. Players or jugglers from the Carnatic.
n. m. Players from the Carnatic.

s. m. A supercargo, pilot or helmsman.

s.m. Name of a mountain, one of the seven principal ranges of mountains which divide the universe.

ety: S. करणी; or करणीयं, or fem. of karnā, q.v.
def: s.f. An action, act, deed, doing; business.
more: n. f. S. कृ to do. 1. An act (فعل). 1. Jaisī karnī vaisī bharnī . Prov. As you do, enjoy or rue. 2. Jānan-bāre jānye, mūrakh man pachhtāë, Karnī bhūle āpnī, aur ko dosh lagāè . Dohā. Those who repent are fools, the knowing ones ne'er do, When they do wrong the blame on others they bestow. 3. Keh karnī par hakūmat? Rus. Prov. What have you done so wonderful that you must needs affect to rule? 4. Karnī na dharnī, nām Gulabiyā . E. Prov. Nor deeds nor wealth, a Rose her name. 5. Bāt lākh kī, karnī khāk kī . Prov. Words a hundred thousand, deeds not one. 6. Samajh, man, apnī karnī, ādi, ant nij kāhū na pāval? maddham gati sakal barnī . Kabīr. Of your own deeds, O heart, take heed! No one his origin or end can tell, all speak but of the interval. 7. Terī karnī tere āge, merī karnī mere āge . Prov. For your acts you shall pay, my own will me repay. 2. A trowel. Jab rāj ne qazā ke karnī, basūlī…
s. f. 1. Action, business. 2. A trowel.

n. f. 1. Working; operation; carrying on of a business ( dhandā ). 2. Process; proceeding; procedure. 3. Execution management; conduct; administration. 4. Discharge or performance of work. kār-ravāī baëbāt . Foreclosure proceedings. kār-ravāī sarsarī . Summary procedure. kār-ravāī-i-adālat . Judicial proceedings. kār-ravāī k. , v. a. 1. To execute or discharg a function or duty. 2. ( Law ) To hold proceedings. kār-ravāī multavī rakhnā . To stay proceedings. kār-ravāī-i-nīlām . Sale proceedings. kār-i-roz-marrah , n. m. Daily duty. kār-sāz , adj. Doer; maker. Woh baṛā kār-sāz hai. kār-sāzī , n. f. 1. Work. 2. Organization; adjustment; order. Khudā kī kār-sāzī hai . 3. Dodge; cheating ( chālākī , 4). kār-sāzī se , Unfairly; fraudulently.

more: adj. Black; dark. krishn, Kishan, Kanhaiyā ; Bhoj. Kīsun , Poet. Kānhā, Kān , n. m. A Hin. god, the tenth avatar or incarnation of Vishnu; the Apollo of the Hindus, generally painted blue. krishnār'pan , n. A grant of land to a Brahman or to a temple in propitiation of Krishna. krishn paksh, krishn-pakh, yā pachh , n. m. The fortnight during which the moon is on the wane; the waning half. krishn-jaṭā , n. f. The Indian spikenard; Andropogon nardus. krishn-jīrak , n. m. Colonjī , a plant with a small black seed; Nigella sativa , Wat. krishn-charan, yā chūrā , n. A plant; Poinciana pulcherrima , Wat. krishn-sārā . The black antelope (?). krishn-loh , n. The loadstone. krishnā-phal , n. A fruit; the Corinda.

1. adj. Black, dark blue. 2. n. prop. The deity so named. See کشن kishan. کرشناوتار kr̤ish̤ṇāvatār, The incarnation of the god Vish̤ṇu, in the form of Kr̤ish̤ṇa. کرشن پچهہ कृष्णपत्र ( कृष्ण Black, and पक्ष Half of the lunar month) kr̤ish̤ṇ-pachh̤, or -pakh̤, or -paksh̤, or kr̤ish̤ṇa-paksh̤, s. m. The dark half of a month, the fortnight during which the moon is in the wane. کرشن چرن kr̤ish̤ṇ-charaṇ, s. m. Name of a plant. (Poinciana pulcherrima).کرشن مي kr̤ish̤ṇa-maya, adj. Full or composed of Kr̤ish̤ṇa.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit कृष्ण (kṛṣṇa). Doublet of کنھیا (kanhaiyā, inherited) and کشن (kiśan, semi-learned borrowing).

def: s.m. Doer, maker, author, creator, agent; workman; operator; party (in a cause); proprietor, master, chief; husband; (in Gram.) the nominative case (kartā is commonly used as a suffix to verbal nouns to form nouns of agency, e.g. pālan-kartā, 'cherisher, protector')
more: n. m. S. कृ to do. 1. An agent; doer. 2. Cause; author; creator. 3. ( Gram .) The agent; nominative; subject. 4. ( kartā dhartā ) The active or managing member of a family. kartā putr yā putrī , n. A son or daughter who performs a father's obsequies, and is therefore considered entitled to the inheritance.
adj. Trained; practised; experienced. Kartā ustād, na kartā shāgird . Prov. Who works a master is, who works not but his pupil is.
s. m. 1. Agent, doer, maker, author, creator. 2. Nominative (in gram.). 3. A proprietor, master. 4. A husband.
subs: s.m. A son who performs his father's obsequies, and is therefore considered entitled to the inheritance
s.m. An epithet of Brahmā, or of Vishṇu, or of Śʼiva, as the creator
or kartā, s.m. The active or managing member of a family.

def: adj. To be done, to be made; experimental; — s.m. Deed, act, business; performance; achievement, exploit, feat; experiment; capability; art, skill, genius; sleight of hand, legerdemain, jugglery; (in Math.) a problem, a proposition
more: n. m. S. कर्तृत्व 1. Deed; act; performance. Bahū bātoṅ baṛī, ki kartab! Prov. Is the new wife a great one for talk or for work? 2. Feat; achievement; exploit. Bhāī! raṅgrej bhī kartab ke dhanī sain, dhūmāṅ- gāḍī bin baldāṅ pavan ḍhāl chalā rākhī saë! W. Rus. My good fellow! the English too are proficients in Art. Their steam carriages, without oxen, go like wind! 3. Experiments. 4. Skill; art; tactics; manceuvre. 5. Jugglery; sleight of hand; legerdemain; rope-dancing. 6. (A. shakl-i-savālī ), ( Math .) A problem proposition. kartab biddyā . ( Phys .) Experimental scieno kurtab pīchhe , adv. Ex post facto. kartab dikhānā , v. a. To show great skill. Woh apnī tīr-andāzī ke kartab dikhāne lage .
s. m. 1. Action, business, deed. 2. Skill, art.
